title: VTF to Arrhenius crossover in temperature dependence of conductivity in (PEG)xNH4ClO4 polymer electrolyte
abstract:
VTF to Arrhenius Crossover in Temperature Dependence of
Conductivity in (PEG) xNH4ClO4 Polymer Electrolyte
NADER BINESH, S. V. BHAT
Department of Physics, Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore—560012, India
Received 6 June 1997; revised 10 November 1997; accepted 12 November 1997
ABSTRACT:
We have studied the temperature variation of conductivity and 1H NMR
linewidth of (PEG)xNH4ClO4 (x Å 20, 30, 46, 100, 200, & 1000) polymer electrolyte
systems. The temperature dependence of the conductivity shows two distinct behav-
iors, the low temperature VTF dependence crossing over to Arrhenius dependence at
higher temperatures. The departure from the VTF behavior is found to be composition
dependent. NMR spectra indicate the presence of large fractions of crystalline regions
that start to melt around the crossover temperature. We understand the deviation
from the VTF behavior as a consequence of this crystalline to elastomer transition.
q 1998 John Wiley & Sons, Inc. J Polym Sci B: Polym Phys 36: 1201–1209, 1998
Keywords: polymer electrolytes; NMR; conductivity; glasses; Arrhenius and VTF de-
pendences

We have studied the temperature variation of conductivity and 1H NMR

that start to melt around the crossover temperature. We understand the deviation

ature by TC. We ﬁnd that the VTF equation ﬁts
the region below TC, and the Arrhenius equation
ﬁts the region above TC as shown in the ﬁgure.
